sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something important! After pressing DOWN:
- The right green square (N) moved from rows 30-32 to rows 33-35 (boundary [[33, 42], [33, 44], [35, 44], [35, 42]])
- The right green square's center dot is W (white) at (34, 43)
- The right dark gray square (c) now has 16 pixels (boundary [[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 44]]) - it's fully repaired!
- The left green square (N) is still at rows 30-12, cols 12-14
- The left dark gray square (c) still has 13 pixels (damaged)
Wait, that's interesting. The left green square didn't move (still at rows 30-32, cols 12-14), but the right green square moved down! And the right dark gray square is now fully repaired (16 pixels).
So it seems like:
- Before the MOUSE click: DOWN moved the left green square.
- After the MOUSE click: DOWN moved the right green square!
The MOUSE click swapped which green square is "active" (controlled by arrow keys). Before the click, the left green square was active. After the click, the right green square is active.
Also, the right dark gray square is now fully repaired (16 pixels), which means the right green square being at rows 33-35, cols 42-44 is the correct position to repair it. But wait, the right dark gray square is at rows 26-30, and the right green square is at rows 33-35. They're not overlapping at all!
Hmm, let me reconsider. The right dark gray square's boundary is [[26, 44], [26, 48], [30, 48], [30, 44]], which is a 4x4 square at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The right green square is at rows 33-35, cols 42-44. They don't overlap, but the right dark gray square is repaired.
So the repair mechanism is not about overlapping. Maybe the repair happens when the green square is at a specific position relative to the dark gray square.
Let me think about this differently. The right dark gray square was damaged (15 pixels) when the right green square was at rows 30-32. After moving the right green square down to rows 33-35, the right dark gray square is repaired (16 pixels). So the correct position for the right green square is rows 33-35 (below the right dark gray square).
Similarly, the left dark gray square was repaired (16 pixels) when the left green square was at rows 33-35 (inside the left dark gray square). But now the left green square is at rows 30-32, and the left dark gray square is damaged (13 pixels).
Wait, but the left green square is at rows 30-32, and the left dark gray square is at rows 32-36. They overlap at row 32. The left dark gray square has 13 pixels, which means it's damaged.
So the correct position for the left green square is rows 33-35 (inside the left dark gray square), and the correct position for the right green square is rows 33-35 (below the right dark gray square).
Currently:
- Left green square: rows 30-32 (needs to be at rows 33-35)
- Right green square: rows 33-35 (correct!)
- Right dark gray square: repaired (16 pixels)
- Left dark gray square: damaged (13 pixels)
The right green square is active (controlled by arrow keys) after the MOUSE click. I need to move the left green square down, but the left green square is not active. So I need to click on the left green square to make it active, then press DOWN.
Wait, but the left green square is at rows 30-32, and I need it at rows 33-35. If I click on the left green square to make it active, then press DOWN, it should move to rows 33-35.
But wait, the right green square is currently active. If I press DOWN again, the right green square would move further down (to rows 36-38), which might break the repair of the right dark gray square.
Let me first click on the left green square to make it active, then press DOWN.
